12316:
Nobody asked the artists, photographers, writers, and filmmakers before they scraped our work into a dataset. So this isn't a hype page. It's a working creative's field manual for the synthetic age — how to use these tools without surrendering taste, judgment, authorship, or your community's trust.

The thesis underneath all of it is Both Hands Full: build human capacity (taste, ethics, judgment) at the same time as you build machine capacity (tools, pipelines, automation). One hand without the other is the failure mode.

Start here

  • Both Hands Full — what creatives actually need to know about AI.
  • Your taste is your moat — why judgment beats "creativity" once generation is cheap.
  • Make Culture, Not Content — a field manual for creative pros in the synthetic age.

Practice & workflow

  • Speak it into existence — voice-first AI workflows that capture richer thinking.
  • AI keynote slides need taste before prompts — my visual workflow, slop and all.
  • Agent orchestrators & creative insurgents — orchestrating AI without losing authorship.
  • The AI mindset — thinking with machines without surrendering the human weirdness.

12317:
I show up. To the meetups, the hackathons, the film festivals, and the big global conferences that land in our backyard — usually with my neck out and a point of view. These are the recaps and survival guides: what actually happened in the room, who was building, and what it means for the rest of us.

The throughline: the signal was already here before the lanyards arrived. Events are where the Vancouver AI community gets loud in public.

12318:
Most AI conversations force a binary. Boosters say embrace it. Doomers say resist it. Both feel lazy. I hold the critique in one hand and the capability in the other — and I think that's the only honest place to stand right now. This is where I work through the hard parts: power, bias, consent, sovereignty, and what we actually owe each other in the synthetic age.

Start here

  • Punk Rock AI — holding the critique and the capability at the same time.
  • Name the Bias — stop saying "AI bias"; name what you're actually seeing.
  • You Can't Drink Data — notes from my first anti-data-centre protest.

Power, policy & sovereignty

  • Canada's AI for All strategy — a skeptical guide to the hype, the money, and the missing guardrails.
  • Applied Ethical AI — why we built the Responsible AI Professional certification.
  • AI for the People — why open source is the future of tech sovereignty.
  • The Great Creative Reckoning — a techartist's manifesto on generative AI.

This is where I help journalists, PR pros, and media people get their hands dirty with AI now, on their own terms, before the tools get used on them instead of by them.

12322:
As a white settler on the journey of allyship, I use my voice and my platform to amplify Indigenous leaders building technology and economies on their own terms — on unceded territory, rooted in respect, cultural integrity, and sovereignty. In America there's "Big Balls." Here in Canada, there's Big Balance.

Indigenous innovation & sovereignty

  • How Indigenomics AI is flipping the script — reimagining what economics measures.
  • Indigenomics NOW 2024 — Indigenous economic and digital sovereignty through AI.
  • Indigenous innovation in technology — leaders blending tradition and tech on Bay Street.
  • The Global Indigenous Technology House — Carol Anne Hilton's vision for the future.
